channel.title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
channel.description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

item.title: Iran Arrests Japanese NHK Bureau Chief Amid Systematic Press Crackdown
item.description: Iranian authorities arrested Japanese journalist Shinnosuke Kawashima and implemented comprehensive surveillance tactics including device confiscation and internet shutdowns during recent protest crackdown.

item.title: Nawat Indigenous Language Gets Official Wikipedia After 16-Year Incubation
item.description: Wikipedia in Nawat, a critically endangered indigenous language of El Salvador, achieved official status in January 2026 after community mobilization focused on digital sovereignty and knowledge preservation.

item.title: Rights Groups Demand Transparency from AppLogic Networks Over Egypt Censorship Tech
item.description: Human rights orgs are demanding AppLogic Networks prove they've actually withdrawn deep packet inspection tech from Egypt after years of facilitating mass censorship of 630+ websites including independent media.

item.title: State Actors Weaponize Consumer Camera Hijacking During Military Operations
item.description: New research reveals Iranian state hackers hijacked hundreds of consumer cameras coordinated with missile strikes, with Israel, Russia, and Ukraine adopting similar surveillance warfare tactics.

item.title: Tenth Circuit Overturns Overbroad Digital Searches of Protesters
item.description: Federal appeals court ruled Colorado Springs police violated Fourth Amendment with sweeping device searches of housing protester, denying qualified immunity in rare victory for digital privacy rights.

item.title: EFF Challenges Warrantless Border Device Searches in Third Circuit Appeal
item.description: EFF filed amicus brief urging Third Circuit to require warrants for border device searches, citing 55,318 CBP device searches in FY2025 and arguing digital data searches exceed original border exception purpose.

item.title: CBP Admits Using Online Ad Systems for Warrantless Location Tracking
item.description: Customs and Border Protection confirmed using real-time bidding (RTB) ad data to track phone locations without warrants, exposing how advertising surveillance enables government surveillance.

item.title: Iran Deploys Two-Tiered 'Digital Apartheid' Internet During 2026 Protests
item.description: Iran implemented its most severe internet shutdown ever in 2026, featuring a 'class-based' system with privileged 'white SIM cards' for loyalists while completely disconnecting ordinary citizens during protests.

item.title: US Senate Bill Blocks Federal Reserve CBDC Issuance Until 2031
item.description: US Senate housing bill includes provision prohibiting Federal Reserve from issuing a Central Bank Digital Currency through beginning of 2031, blocking government digital currency surveillance infrastructure.

item.title: CBP Confirms Use of Ad Tech Data for Warrantless Location Surveillance
item.description: CBP admits it uses location data from online advertising systems (RTB) to track phones without warrants, confirming the advertising surveillance infrastructure enables government spying.

item.title: Iran Maintains 98% Internet Shutdown During Military Conflict, Highlighting Need for D2C Satellite
item.description: Iran has blocked 98% of internet connectivity for 12+ days during U.S./Israel military attacks, prompting calls for Direct-to-Cell satellite deployment to bypass government shutdowns.

item.title: Federal Agents Arrest Independent Photographer, Seize Equipment Over ICE Protest Coverage
item.description: DOJ charged independent photographer Shane Ryan Bollman with felony civil rights violations for covering an anti-ICE protest, with HSI agents seizing his devices without warrant.

item.title: Israel Bans Five Palestinian News Outlets as 'Terrorist Organizations'
item.description: Israel designated five Palestinian news outlets as terrorist organizations, forcing suspensions and journalist arrests. No evidence provided for Hamas links allegations.

item.title: Civil Rights Groups Challenge Surveillance Firm's Rebrand from Sandvine to AppLogic Networks
item.description: Access Now and other rights groups demand transparency from AppLogic (formerly Sandvine) after its removal from US Entity List, citing lack of remedy for DPI surveillance abuses in Egypt.

item.title: Cake Wallet Adds Self-Custody Lightning Network With Privacy Defaults
item.description: Cake Wallet integrated Bitcoin Lightning Network with self-custody and privacy-first design using Breez SDK and custom Spark implementation, avoiding common Lightning privacy leaks.

item.title: Austrian Interior Ministry Deploys €900K Mass Surveillance Tool Used by ICE
item.description: Austria's Interior Ministry spent €900K on Cobwebs' Tangles surveillance software that harvests social media and web data, with officials refusing to disclose usage details or capabilities.

item.title: Research Exposes EU Agencies' Private Surveillance Partnerships Targeting Migrants
item.description: Access Now research reveals 450+ companies pitch surveillance tech to EU agencies via exclusive industry events, building biometric databases and AI systems that bypass data protection laws.

item.title: Utexo Raises $7.5M for Bitcoin-Native USDT Settlement with Privacy Features
item.description: Utexo secured $7.5M led by Tether to build Bitcoin-native USDT settlement infrastructure with encrypted transactions and sub-second settlement, abstracting Lightning Network complexity behind a single API.

item.title: Federal Agents Arrest Journalist, Seize Electronics Without Warrant in ICE Protest Coverage
item.description: DOJ charged independent photographer Junn Bollmann with felony civil rights violations for covering anti-ICE protest, confiscating his devices without warrant—part of broader crackdown on press freedom.

item.title: LLMs Enable Scalable De-anonymization of Online Posts Across Platforms
item.description: Research shows LLMs can identify anonymous users from their online posts with high precision across platforms like Hacker News and Reddit, scaling to tens of thousands of candidates.

item.title: Angola Enacts Sweeping Civil Society Restrictions, Expands State Control Over Media
item.description: Angola's president signed legislation granting government broad powers to monitor, suspend, and restrict NGOs under vague 'security threat' provisions, part of expanding crackdown on civil society and media.

item.title: CPJ Documents Systematic ICE Targeting of Journalists During Second Trump Administration
item.description: Immigration authorities detained Tennessee journalist Estefany Rodríguez despite legal status, part of documented pattern targeting press covering ICE activities and immigration issues.

item.title: Proton Releases Comprehensive Protest Privacy Guide Against AI Surveillance
item.description: Proton published detailed operational security guide for protesters facing AI facial recognition, biometric tracking, and mobile surveillance tools like ICE's Mobile Fortify app.

item.title: DRC Journalists Detained, Broadcasters Occupied as Warring Parties Control Information Flow
item.description: Government forces and M23 rebels in eastern DRC systematically detain journalists and occupy broadcast stations to control war coverage, creating information blackout zones.

item.title: Trump Admin Uses Federal Agencies to Target Critical Media and Anonymous Activists
item.description: Federal agencies launch retaliatory investigations against Media Matters, journalists, and anonymous critics, including seizure of Signal account with 1,000+ sources and subpoenas to unmask anti-ICE activists.

item.title: BIP-360 Proposes Quantum Defense Upgrades for Bitcoin
item.description: BIP-360 introduces quantum resistance improvements to Bitcoin's security model, though it falls short of providing complete post-quantum protection.

item.title: Greek Court Convicts Spyware Executives in Landmark Predatorgate Ruling
item.description: Athens court found four Intellexa spyware executives guilty of unlawfully targeting journalists and politicians with Predator spyware, marking the first criminal accountability for surveillance tech companies in Greece.

item.title: Anonymous Credentials: Privacy-Preserving Authentication for Age Verification Era
item.description: Matthew Green explores anonymous credentials as a solution to maintain privacy while complying with new age verification laws across 25 US states and multiple countries that threaten to create citizen-level online surveillance.

item.title: Cambodia Sentences Two Journalists to 14 Years for Border Conflict Photography
item.description: Cambodian court sentenced two journalists to 14 years for treason after photographing soldiers near disputed Thai border, part of broader press freedom crackdown targeting at least four journalists since July 2025.

item.title: Wisconsin Removes VPN Ban from Age Verification Bill After Pushback
item.description: Wisconsin lawmakers removed VPN bans from age verification bill S.B. 130/A.B. 105 after widespread opposition, though EFF still opposes the bill awaiting Governor Evers' signature.

item.title: Access Now Launches Spear Phishing Defense Webinar Series for Civil Society
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline is launching a webinar series on March 10, 2026, starting with spear phishing defense training for activists, journalists, and civil society organizations.

item.title: Florida Proposes State Spy Unit to 'Neutralize' People Based on Views and Opinions
item.description: Florida legislature advances bill creating state counterintelligence unit that could target individuals whose 'views or opinions are a threat' to the state, raising COINTELPRO-style surveillance concerns.

item.title: Columbia University Failed to Protect Students from Warrantless ICE Raids Despite Clear Policies
item.description: ICE agents repeatedly entered Columbia University housing without warrants using deception, with school security failing to enforce policies requiring judicial warrants for non-public areas.

item.title: EFF Warns of Growing Age Verification Mandates and Facial Recognition Surveillance
item.description: EFF highlights escalating threats to online privacy including Discord's mandatory age verification, Meta's face-scanning smart glasses, and surveillance tech normalization.

item.title: ICE Detains Colombian Journalist Covering Immigration Enforcement in Nashville
item.description: ICE detained journalist Estefany Rodríguez, who covers immigration enforcement for Nashville Notícias, despite being in the US legally with pending asylum and green card applications.

item.title: Bitcoin Crosses $1T Threshold as Institutional ETFs Drive Market Maturation
item.description: Bitcoin has evolved from experimental asset to macro-scale financial instrument with $1T+ market cap, driven by institutional ETFs absorbing 6.3% of supply and fundamentally changing market dynamics.

item.title: EFF Honors Women Digital Rights Pioneers on International Women's Day
item.description: EFF staff highlight influential women in digital rights, from NSA surveillance fighter Cindy Cohn to the Jane Collective's 1970s abortion access infosec project that pioneered modern movement security practices.

item.title: Pentagon Rejects AI Ethics Constraints, Enables Autonomous Weapons Development
item.description: US Defense Department rejected Anthropic's ethical red lines against fully autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, signing with OpenAI instead under new 'any lawful use' requirements.

item.title: ICE Uses Biometric Surveillance to Stalk Legal Observers to Their Homes
item.description: Federal immigration agents are using advanced surveillance tools including Mobile Fortify biometric app to identify legal observers and intimidate them by following them home, chilling First Amendment monitoring activities.

item.title: EFF Confirms Government Using Ad Surveillance Networks to Access User Data
item.description: EFF confirms government agencies are tapping into advertising surveillance infrastructure to collect citizen data, including CBP using targeted ads to track locations.

item.title: Kenya Court Strikes Down 'Fake News' Law Sections, Upholds Surveillance Powers
item.description: Kenya's Court of Appeal nullified cybercrime law sections criminalizing 'false news' publication but upheld cyber harassment and surveillance provisions affecting digital rights.

item.title: Taliban Shuts Down and Seizes Private TV Station in Afghanistan
item.description: Taliban intelligence raided and forcibly closed Rah-e-Farda TV in Kabul, seizing assets and expelling staff after owner criticized Taliban-Pakistan conflict.

item.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Training Series for Civil Society
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline is launching a webinar series starting March 10, 2026, focusing on spear phishing defense for activists, journalists, and civil society organizations facing targeted attacks.

item.title: Schneier Advocates for Canadian Public AI to Counter U.S. Tech Dependence
item.description: Security expert Bruce Schneier argues Canada should build public AI infrastructure instead of relying on U.S. companies like OpenAI, citing Switzerland's Apertus model as proof of concept.

item.title: Turkey Weaponizes Justice System Against Leading Opposition Politician
item.description: Istanbul mayor and presidential candidate Ekrem İmamoğlu faces mass corruption trial March 9, after year in detention as Turkey systematically targets opposition party through courts.

item.title: CBP Uses Ad Data for Phone Tracking; Proton Helps FBI ID Protester
item.description: US border agency CBP used online advertising data to track phone locations, while encrypted email provider Proton assisted FBI in identifying a protester.

item.title: Access Now Maps 450 Companies Building EU's Migration Surveillance Infrastructure
item.description: Access Now investigation reveals 450+ private companies pitched 600+ surveillance technologies to EU migration agencies, creating mass surveillance system targeting migrants with limited oversight.

item.title: Panama Court Order Blocks Journalists from Reporting on Sanctioned Contractors
item.description: Panamanian prosecutor issued 4-month gag order against journalists investigating state contractors later sanctioned by US Treasury for supporting Maduro regime.

item.title: Turkey Systematically Targets Opposition Leader İmamoğlu in Mass Trial
item.description: Istanbul mayor and presidential candidate Ekrem İmamoğlu faces politically motivated mass trial with 407 defendants, part of 17-month campaign to remove opposition leadership through weaponized justice system.

item.title: EFF Highlights Women Pioneers in Digital Rights and Security Culture
item.description: EFF celebrates women who shaped digital rights, from NSA surveillance fighters to the Jane Collective's analog security practices that pioneered compartmentalization and secure deletion for activists.

item.title: Chinese Whistleblower Exposes Surveillance Operations in China and US
item.description: A Chinese whistleblower has revealed details about China's surveillance operations targeting citizens domestically and in the United States, according to CNN reporting.

item.title: Taliban Seizes Private TV Station, Continues Media Crackdown in Afghanistan
item.description: Taliban intelligence raided and shut down Rah-e-Farda TV in Kabul, seizing assets and expelling staff, continuing systematic suppression of independent media in Afghanistan.

item.title: ICE Detains Journalist Reporting on Immigration Enforcement
item.description: ICE detained Colombian journalist Estefany Rodríguez who covered immigration enforcement for Nashville Notícias, with legal team claiming retaliation for her reporting.

item.title: Belarus Sentences Radio Host to 3 Years for 'Extremist' Journalism Activities
item.description: Belarusian court sentenced radio journalist Aleh Khamenka to 3 years in prison for 'extremist activities' related to his work with Poland-based Radio Racyja, marking the third journalist conviction in under a week.

item.title: Iran's Total Internet Shutdown Enables Mass State Violence During 2026 Uprising
item.description: Iran imposed a 15-day total internet blackout during nationwide protests, enabling unprecedented state violence with 30,000+ deaths while regime-aligned voices abroad filled the information vacuum to shape international narratives.

item.title: LLMs Enable Large-Scale Deanonymization of Online Posts
item.description: New research shows LLM agents can identify anonymous users across platforms like Reddit and Hacker News with high precision, scaling to tens of thousands of candidates by inferring location, occupation, and interests.

item.title: Pakistani Academic Sentenced to Death for Facebook Posts Spends 13 Years in Solitary
item.description: Junaid Hafeez has been imprisoned for 13 years, mostly in solitary confinement, after being sentenced to death for alleged blasphemous Facebook posts, highlighting Pakistan's systematic suppression of online speech.

item.title: 40 Uyghurs Forcibly Returned to China by Thailand Remain Missing After One Year
item.description: Thailand deported 40 Uyghur men to China in February 2025 despite torture risks. Their whereabouts remain unknown, constituting forced disappearances under international law.

item.title: Tibet: 45 Detained for Digital Communications as China Intensifies Phone Surveillance
item.description: Chinese authorities detained 45 Tibetans in 2024-25, primarily for phone content including sharing information abroad or having Dalai Lama materials, with random phone searches as key surveillance tactic.

item.title: Vietnam Imprisons Women Activists for Digital Dissent and Rights Advocacy
item.description: Vietnam has imprisoned four prominent women activists for 7-9 years each for online blogging, protesting cybersecurity laws, and advocating for rights, highlighting the repressive environment where freedom tech becomes essential.

item.title: Mexico Partially Unblocks Tor Access After Years of Government Website Censorship
item.description: Mexico's new administration under Claudia Sheinbaum partially lifted a Tor network block affecting 20+ million users, allowing access to main government sites while maintaining restrictions elsewhere.

item.title: Iran Internet Shutdowns Impact Diaspora Family Communications
item.description: Iranian internet restrictions are forcing diaspora families to rely on messaging app read receipts as vital signs of life for relatives inside the country.

item.title: Trump Admin Cuts Freedom Tech Funding While Launching 'Anti-Censorship' Portal
item.description: Trump administration launches freedom.gov to bypass European regulations while simultaneously cutting funding to organizations that build censorship circumvention tools and support at-risk activists globally.

item.title: EFF: OpenAI's Pentagon Deal Contains Surveillance Loopholes Despite Public Assurances
item.description: EFF analysis reveals OpenAI's amended Pentagon contract uses "weasel words" like "intentionally" and "unconstrained" that could enable mass surveillance of US persons despite public promises.

item.title: EFF Challenges Geofence Warrants at Supreme Court as Unconstitutional Digital Dragnets
item.description: EFF filed Supreme Court brief in Chatrie v. United States arguing geofence warrants violate Fourth Amendment by compelling mass location data searches of innocent people without individualized suspicion.

item.title: Iran's Internet Connectivity Drops 99% Amid Strikes and Shutdowns
item.description: Iran has cut internet connectivity by 99%, with air strikes causing additional outages and circumvention tools reportedly failing to provide reliable access.

item.title: Iran's DPI-Powered Internet Whitelisting System Built with Russian Tech Support
item.description: Iran deployed infrastructure-level internet whitelisting using Deep Packet Inspection during 2025-26 protests, with Russian company Protei providing DPI systems to major Iranian telecoms, enabling granular traffic control beyond previous shutdowns.

item.title: Anthropic Sues Pentagon Over AI Surveillance Mandate, EFF Backs First Amendment Defense
item.description: Anthropic is challenging the Pentagon's designation of the company as a 'supply chain risk' after refusing to remove AI guardrails for government surveillance, with EFF arguing this violates First Amendment protections.

item.title: Nawat Language Wikipedia Launches After 16-Year Journey to Digital Sovereignty
item.description: El Salvador's critically endangered Nawat language launched its own Wikipedia in 2026 after community mobilization by Indigenous speakers seeking digital sovereignty and knowledge preservation.

item.title: EFF Executive Director Discusses 30-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance in New Book
item.description: EFF's Cindy Cohn hosts livestream March 19th to discuss her new book 'Privacy's Defender' covering three decades of legal battles against NSA, FBI, and Justice Department surveillance.

item.title: Pro-Palestine Student Activist Fights Deportation Despite Government Lacking Evidence
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil faces deportation to Algeria for pro-Palestine activism at Columbia, with newly unsealed DHS memo showing government lacked evidence but proceeded anyway as retaliation for speech.

item.title: Intellexa Executives Sentenced to 8 Years for Greek Predator Spyware Scandal
item.description: Four Intellexa executives including the founder received 8-year prison sentences for deploying Predator spyware against 90+ Greek public figures - the first criminal conviction of mercenary spyware company leadership.

item.title: EFF Releases Guide to Expose Police Surveillance Tech Sales Hype
item.description: EFF, CJJ, and IPVM released 'Selling Safety' report helping journalists critically examine police surveillance technology claims and expose industry tactics that exploit taxpayers while eroding civil liberties.

item.title: Building Self-Hosted Communities: Taiwan's Response to Platform Censorship
item.description: Shin Yang built Lezismore, an independent self-hosted platform for Taiwan's sexual minorities, after mainstream platforms like Facebook drove LGBTQ+ communities to self-censor or face removal.

item.title: EFF Fights Legal Challenge That Could Make Website Embedding Illegal
item.description: EFF filed amicus brief defending 'server test' that protects website embedding from copyright liability. Publisher wants to make link embedding directly liable for infringement.

item.title: Access Now Submits Legal Challenge to Kazakhstan Court Over 2022 Internet Shutdowns
item.description: Digital rights organization Access Now has filed a submission to Kazakhstan's Constitutional Court challenging the legality of internet shutdowns that occurred in 2022.

item.title: OpenAI Wins Pentagon Contract After Anthropic Exit, Won't Release Surveillance Terms
item.description: OpenAI secured Pentagon contract after Anthropic was forced out for refusing mass surveillance terms. Company claims anti-surveillance protections but won't release contract details for verification.

item.title: Proton Launches Lumo: Private AI Alternative After ChatGPT Surveillance Concerns
item.description: Proton released Lumo, a privacy-focused AI assistant with end-to-end encryption and no data logging, following reports that 4M users pledged to leave ChatGPT over government surveillance partnerships.

item.title: DHS Deploys Surveillance Tech Against Activists While Expanding Election Authority
item.description: Department of Homeland Security is using facial recognition and surveillance databases to target activists while an election denier leads DHS election integrity efforts, raising concerns about 2026 midterm interference.

item.title: Popular Iranian Prayer App Hacked for US-Israeli Propaganda Messages
item.description: A prayer app with 5M+ downloads was reportedly compromised by US/Israeli forces to send propaganda messages to Iranians during recent explosions, demonstrating state-level mobile app infiltration capabilities.

item.title: Anonymous Credentials: Privacy Tech for the Age Verification Era
item.description: Cryptographer Matthew Green explains anonymous credentials as essential privacy tech to combat growing government ID verification requirements across 25 US states and multiple countries that threaten online anonymity.

item.title: EFF Honors Women Digital Rights Pioneers on International Women's Day
item.description: EFF celebrates women who shaped digital rights, from NSA surveillance fighter Cindy Cohn to the Jane abortion collective's analog infosec practices that pioneered modern movement security culture.

item.title: EU Mandates Crypto Platform Reporting Starting 2026
item.description: EU implementing new crypto tax rules requiring platforms to report user data and transactions to authorities, significantly reducing financial privacy for digital assets starting 2026.

item.title: Journalists Use Satellite Links, Encrypted Apps to Report from Internet-Cut Iran
item.description: After Iran cut internet access following strikes on officials, journalists are using satellite connections and encrypted messaging to circumvent state censorship and continue reporting.

item.title: Lao Government Critic Found Dead After Arrest, Highlighting Digital Activist Risks
item.description: Sisay Luangmonda, a prominent social media critic of Laos government, was found dead after being arrested by soldiers, part of systematic repression of digital dissent in authoritarian regimes.

item.title: EFF Challenges Geofence Warrants at Supreme Court as Unconstitutional Digital Dragnets
item.description: EFF and civil liberties groups filed Supreme Court brief arguing geofence warrants violate Fourth Amendment by turning location data into mass surveillance dragnets that make suspects of innocent bystanders.

item.title: Iran War Triggers Massive Internet Blackout, Systematic Media Suppression Across Middle East
item.description: Iran imposed near-total internet blackout (down to 1% connectivity) as war began, while multiple states targeted journalists, bombed media facilities, and restricted press coverage across the region.

item.title: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn Releases Memoir on 30-Year Digital Privacy Fight
item.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn publishes memoir documenting three decades of digital privacy battles including Crypto Wars, NSA surveillance, and FBI gag orders as she prepares to step down.

item.title: EFF Fights Copyright Abuse to Protect Anonymous Religious Dissent
item.description: Watch Tower is using DMCA subpoenas to unmask anonymous Jehovah's Witnesses who created research tools exposing church document changes and failed prophecies.

item.title: Iranian Prayer App Hacked to Send Surrender Messages During Israeli Strikes
item.description: Iranians received push notifications promising amnesty and saying 'help is on the way' through a compromised prayer app during Israeli airstrikes on Tehran.

item.title: Iran Internet Blackout Isolates 15 Jailed Journalists During Regional Conflict
item.description: Iran imposed near-total internet blackout during regional escalation, cutting off at least 15 imprisoned journalists from outside contact, with three held at Evin prison in Israeli-ordered evacuation zone.

item.title: Netherlands Considers Jailbreaking F-35 Jets to Break US Software Dependency
item.description: Dutch Defense Secretary said he could jailbreak F-35 fighter jets to accept third-party software, highlighting growing concerns about US technological dependencies.

item.title: Bitcoin Core IBD Optimization Critical for Maintaining Decentralized Node Access
item.description: Bitcoin Core developers continuously optimize Initial Block Download to prevent cheap consumer hardware from being priced out of running full nodes as blockchain grows.

item.title: Sophisticated iPhone Hacking Toolkit Linked to US Government Infects Tens of Thousands
item.description: A highly advanced iPhone exploitation toolkit, likely developed for the US government, has reportedly infected tens of thousands of devices in a mass surveillance operation.

item.title: Far-Right Think Tank Helped Craft DOJ Antifa Terror Indictment Against ICE Protesters
item.description: Researcher from Islamophobic think tank provided language for first-ever domestic terrorism case against alleged antifa cell, citing Signal use and security practices as evidence.

item.title: EFF Executive Director Releases Privacy Rights Memoir 'Privacy's Defender'
item.description: EFF's Cindy Cohn is launching a book chronicling her 30-year battle for digital privacy rights, covering crypto wars, surveillance state resistance, and EFF's evolution into a major digital rights organization.

item.title: Lugano Expands Bitcoin Infrastructure with $6.3M Plan ₿ Phase II
item.description: Tether and Lugano launch Phase II expansion targeting digital sovereignty through decentralized infrastructure, privacy-preserving identity, and AI agents by 2030.

item.title: Gov't Uses Privacy Tools as Evidence in First Domestic Terror Case Against Antifa Cell
item.description: Federal prosecutors worked with far-right think tank researcher to craft terror charges against Texas activists, citing use of Signal and security practices as evidence of antifa membership.

item.title: Zimbabwe Escalates Crackdown on Opposition to Presidential Term Extension
item.description: Zimbabwean authorities are using violence, intimidation, and surveillance against civil society groups opposing a constitutional amendment that would extend President Mnangagwa's rule until 2030.

item.title: Florida Bill Creates State Spy Unit to 'Neutralize' Those with Threatening 'Views or Opinions'
item.description: Florida legislation would create a state counterintelligence unit empowered to track and 'neutralize' people whose 'views or opinions' threaten the state, raising COINTELPRO-style civil liberties concerns.

item.title: US Treasury Acknowledges Legitimate Uses of Crypto Mixers in Congressional Report
item.description: U.S. Treasury told Congress that crypto mixers serve legitimate privacy purposes, marking a significant policy shift while proposing new 'hold law' for suspicious digital assets.

item.title: Iran's 98% Internet Shutdown Drives Push for Direct-to-Cell Satellite Tech
item.description: Iran imposed a near-total internet blackout during conflict, prompting digital rights advocates to urgently call for Direct-to-Cell satellite technology that connects smartphones directly to satellites, bypassing government shutdowns.

item.title: EFF Analysis: SAFE Act Proposes Major Section 702 Surveillance Reforms
item.description: The bipartisan SAFE Act would require FBI warrants for accessing Americans' communications collected via Section 702, address parallel construction, and partially close data broker loopholes.

item.title: Ethiopia Shutters Independent Media Outlets Ahead of National Elections
item.description: Ethiopian authorities revoked the license of Addis Standard, one of the country's last independent news outlets, three months before national elections, escalating systematic media suppression.

item.title: Coalition Demands California Revoke Federal ALPR Surveillance Permits on Border Highways
item.description: EFF-led coalition demands California revoke permits allowing CBP and DEA to deploy automated license plate readers disguised as traffic barrels on border highways, citing mass surveillance risks.

item.title: Azerbaijani Journalist Under Armed Surveillance in Switzerland
item.description: Exiled Azerbaijani journalist Emin Huseynov reports being followed by armed men in Switzerland, highlighting transnational repression risks for freedom tech builders and activists.

item.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Webinar Series Targeting Civil Society Spear-Phishing Threats
item.description: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline launches webinar series starting March 2026, focusing on spear-phishing defense for activists and civil society with expert speakers from Citizen Lab and security industry.

item.title: Freedom House Documents Artists Imprisoned for Creative Dissent Across 7 Authoritarian States
item.description: Freedom House catalogs political imprisonment of artists in Russia, Belarus, Cuba, Azerbaijan, Egypt, China, and Venezuela, highlighting systematic state repression of creative expression that challenges power.

item.title: Anonymous Credentials: Privacy-Preserving Auth Against Age Verification Laws
item.description: Cryptographer Matthew Green explains anonymous credentials as essential defense against privacy dystopia created by age verification laws requiring government ID for most online activities.

item.title: EFF Warns Against Meta Smart Glasses Over Privacy and Surveillance Concerns
item.description: EFF raises alarm about Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses, highlighting that footage is reviewed by human contractors, used for AI training, and enables covert surveillance of bystanders.
